calls.comment: GW_CharacterStat::petLockerSN (DecodeBuffer 24 bytes = 3 × int64; atlas for-loop body collapses to 1 static entry)

dwSwallowCharacterID — conditional: only if destroyType == 4 (swallowed by character/eater)

CMobPool::OnMobChangeController.notes: Production wire shape only — v95 dev builds (CClientOptMan::GetOpt(2)) prepend an extra 12-byte moveRandSeed block before dwMobID. Atlas server never sends seeds (server-side never sets opt 2) so atlas's encoder is correct for production. Seeds previously listed here caused false-❌ diff misalignments; removed since production traffic never carries them. SetLocalMob's Decode4(templateId) + MonsterModel body when controlMode != 0 is delegated and not expanded inline; atlas writes WriteByte(5) at the aggro position (hardcoded; semantic but not wire concern) + WriteInt(monsterId) + MonsterModel.

randTimeForAreaAttack[i] — per entry, loop nCount times

slot — read by CUser::OnPetPacket before dispatch
excluded item id — per entry, loop nCount times

itemId per entry — loop nCount times
itemId or meso amount (atlas: meso if isMeso else itemId)

flags byte (atlas dwFlag — multiBitFlag with nMoveType / bRiseByToss / etc.)
pet pos.x — conditional: only when dwID % 13 == 0
mode/sub-op byte: 3=SHOW, 4=SEND_SUCCESS, 5=SEND_ERROR, 7=REFRESH — dispatcher; sub-op modelled via #-suffixed synthetic entries
dwSN (memo id) — GW_Memo::Decode loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens)
mode (=4, SEND_SUCCESS) — no further bytes; success path shows UI notification only
mode (=7, REFRESH) — no further bytes; calls OnMemoNotify_Receive internally to send REQUEST packet
op (=2, REQUEST) — byte sent as NOTE_ACTION sub-op by OnMemoNotify_Receive@0x9f3830
count — number of checked (nFlag==3) memos to discard
emptySlotCount — free inventory slots (tab 4, from CharacterData::GetEmptySlotCount)
dwSN (memo serial number) — per-entry loop body (count iterations; analyzer flattens)
senderName (gift sender name — from gift list entry)
mode (=0x15/21, CAPACITY_UPDATE) — dispatcher switch byte consumed by OnFriendResult
nFriendMax — new buddy-list capacity written into CharacterData.nFriendMax
mode (=0x14/20, CHANNEL_CHANGE) — dispatcher switch byte consumed by OnFriendResult
dwFriendID — characterId of the buddy whose channel changed
inShop — 0=not in cash shop, 1=in cash shop (m_aInShop[Index])
nChannelID — new channel id (stored in m_aFriend[Index].nChannelID; -1=offline)

mode — error sub-op byte (0x0B=11..0x0F=15, 0x10=16..0x13=19, 0x16=22, 0x17=23); each arm shows a StringPool notice dialog with no further packet reads except cases 0x10/0x11/0x13/0x16 which do Decode1 + optional DecodeStr
mode (=0x09, INVITE) — dispatcher switch byte consumed by OnFriendResult
dwFriendID — originatorId (characterId of the player sending the friend request)
originatorName — display name of the requester (ZXString, variable length)
v25 — extra 4-byte field decoded before GW_Friend::Insert (purpose unknown; possibly job/level)
v26 — extra 4-byte field decoded before GW_Friend::Insert (purpose unknown; possibly job/level)
GW_Friend entry (39 bytes via CFriend::Insert → GW_Friend::Decode → DecodeBuffer(this,0x27)): dwFriendID(4)+sFriendName(13)+nFlag(1)+nChannelID(4)+sFriendGroup(17)
inShop — after GW_Friend::Insert, CFriend::Insert reads Decode1 for m_aInShop[new_entry]
mode (=0x07/0x0A/0x12, LIST_UPDATE variants) — dispatcher switch byte consumed by OnFriendResult

mode (=0x08, UPDATE) — dispatcher switch byte consumed by OnFriendResult
dwFriendID — characterId used to locate the buddy via CFriend::FindIndex

inShop — updated inShop flag stored in m_aInShop[Index]
op (=1, ADD) — sub-op byte for BUDDYLIST_MODIFY (opcode 153)
sTarget — name of the character to add as buddy
op (=2, ACCEPT) — sub-op byte for BUDDYLIST_MODIFY (opcode 153)
dwFriendID — characterId of the buddy whose request is being accepted
op (=3, DELETE) — sub-op byte for BUDDYLIST_MODIFY (opcode 153)
dwFriendID — characterId of the buddy to remove

CUIMessenger::OnCreate.note: client accepts a messenger invite; sends op=0 + messengerId. Op-byte consumed by atlas Operation dispatcher; sub-handler sees only messengerId.

calls.comment: messengerId — room id passed in pData (the invite's room id); op byte (=0) stripped by atlas Operation dispatcher

CUIMessenger::OnDestroy.note: client closes the messenger window; sends op=2 only. Op-byte is the entire payload — handled by atlas Operation struct directly.

calls.comment: op (=2, LEAVE) — only field; atlas Operation struct reads this as mode byte

CUIMessenger::SendInviteMsg.note: client invites a character by name; sends op=3 + targetName. Op-byte consumed by dispatcher; sub-handler sees only targetCharacter.

calls.comment: sTarget — target character name to invite; op byte (=3) stripped by atlas Operation dispatcher

CFadeWnd::SendCloseMessage.note: CFadeWnd type=0 declines a messenger invite; sends op=5 + fromName + myName + pad. Op-byte consumed by dispatcher; sub-handler sees fromName + myName + pad.

calls.comment: m_sInviter (fromName) — name of the character who sent the invite; op byte (=5) stripped by atlas Operation dispatcher

CUIMessenger::ProcessChat.note: client sends a chat message; sends op=6 + chatLine. Op-byte consumed by dispatcher; sub-handler sees only msg.

calls.comment: sText — formatted chat message ('name : msg'); op byte (=6) stripped by atlas Operation dispatcher

CUser::OnChat.note: CHATTEXT (0xB5) / CHATTEXT1 (0xB6). characterId (Decode4) is consumed by CUserPool dispatcher before calling OnChat — not visible here. Sub-op mode byte (Decode1 line 65): 0=normal, 1=npc-name. Then DecodeStr(msg), Decode1(bOnlyBalloon). Atlas GeneralChat writes characterId+gm+msg+show; the characterId is the dispatcher prefix, gm maps to the mode byte, show maps to bOnlyBalloon.

calls.comment: isGM / chat type byte (0=normal player, 1=npc-name; consumed as gm flag in atlas)
bOnlyBalloon (show flag: 0=show in chat log, 1=balloon only)

CField::SendChatMsg.note: GENERAL_CHAT (0x36). Client sends: Encode4(update_time) + EncodeStr(msg) + Encode1(bOnlyBalloon). Atlas General.Encode writes updateTime (GMS>83 gate) + msg + bOnlyBalloon — matches v95 wire exactly.

calls.comment: update_time (get_update_time(); gated GMS>83 in atlas — v95 always present)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Created.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=8 (CREATE). Server sends: Decode4(partyId) + Decode4(townPortalFromId) + Decode4(townPortalToId) + Decode2(x) + Decode2(y). Client sets local party state. Atlas Created writes mode+partyId+2×EmptyMapId+2×shorts.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Invite.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=4 (INVITE). Server sends: Decode4(partyId) + DecodeStr(inviterName) + Decode4(inviterJobId) + Decode4(inviterLevel) + Decode1(autoJoinFlag). Atlas Invite writes mode+partyId+name+0x00 — missing inviterJobId, inviterLevel fields.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Disband.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=12 with isForced=0 path (DISBAND/LEAVE). Reads: Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(targetId)+Decode1(isForced). Atlas Disband writes mode+partyId+targetId+0x00+partyId — the repeated partyId is a known pattern. When isForced=0 and no party data follows, layout matches atlas structure.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Error.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) error sub-modes (9,10,13,17,18,22,29,32-34,36). These modes only display string-pool chat messages; no Decode calls beyond the mode byte consumed by the dispatcher. Atlas Error writes mode+name where name is a server-supplied string. Sub-op enum deferred.

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#ChangeLeader.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=31 (CHANGE_LEADER). Reads: Decode4(newLeaderId) + Decode1(disconnected). Atlas ChangeLeader writes mode+targetCharacterId(4)+disconnected(1). Wire matches.

calls.comment: v67 = disconnected flag (0=normal transfer, 1=due to disconnect)

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Join.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=15 (JOIN). Reads: Decode4(partyId) + DecodeStr(targetName) + PARTYDATA::Decode(378 bytes). Atlas Join writes mode+partyId+name+WritePartyData(298 bytes). WritePartyData is 80 bytes short: missing aTownPortal[6].skillId (24 bytes) + aPQReward/aPQRewardType/dwPQRewardMobTemplateID/bPQReward (56 bytes).

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Left.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=12 with isForced=1 path (LEFT/EXPELLED). Reads: Decode4(partyId)+Decode4(targetId)+Decode1(1)+Decode1(isForced)+DecodeStr(targetName)+PARTYDATA::Decode(378 bytes). Atlas Left writes mode+partyId+targetId+0x01+forced+name+WritePartyData(298 bytes). WritePartyData 80-byte shortfall same as Join/Update.

calls.comment: isForced branch discriminator (1 = member leaving with PARTYDATA)
v38 = forced flag (0=voluntary leave, 1=expelled by leader)
PARTYDATA::Decode(0x17A=378 bytes) — full party state after member left

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#Update.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x3E) mode=7/38 (UPDATE). Reads: Decode4(partyId) + PARTYDATA::Decode(378 bytes). Atlas Update writes mode+partyId+WritePartyData(298 bytes). WritePartyData is 80 bytes short: missing aTownPortal[6].m_nSKillID (6×4=24 bytes) + aPQReward[6](24)+aPQRewardType[6](24)+dwPQRewardMobTemplateID(4)+bPQReward(4)=56 bytes. HOT PATH.

calls.comment: PARTYDATA::Decode(0x17A=378 bytes) — full PARTYDATA struct including PQ reward fields

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#OperationBody.note: Synthetic entry for operation_body.go helper (assembles sub-op packets via WithResolvedCode). No independent wire shape; OP-FAMILY-party sub-op enum deferred to _pending.md.

CUserRemote::OnReceiveHP.note: UPDATE_PARTYMEMBER_HP (0xD6). CUserRemote::OnReceiveHP reads Decode4(hp)+Decode4(maxHp). The characterId is consumed upstream by CUserPool::OnUserRemotePacket before this function is called (dispatcher-prefix pattern). Atlas MemberHP writes WriteInt(characterId)+WriteInt(hp)+WriteInt(maxHp) — characterId is the packet-level prefix prepended by the opcode writer, correct. HOT PATH.

calls.comment: hp — current HP of the remote party member
maxHp — max HP of the remote party member

CField::SendCreateNewPartyMsg.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x91) op=1 (CREATE). Client sends: Encode1(1). No further fields.

CField::SendWithdrawPartyMsg.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x91) op=2 (LEAVE). Client sends: Encode1(2)+Encode1(0). Atlas Operation only carries op byte; the trailing 0x00 is not in atlas OperationJoin/Leave separate struct.

calls.comment: trailing 0 — not modelled in atlas Operation dispatcher

CField::SendKickPartyMsg.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x91) op=5 (EXPEL). Client sends: Encode1(5)+Encode4(targetCharacterId). Atlas OperationExpel writes WriteInt(targetCharacterId) — op byte consumed by dispatcher.

CField::SendChangePartyBossMsg.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x91) op=6 (CHANGE_LEADER). Client sends: Encode1(6)+Encode4(targetCharacterId). Atlas OperationChangeLeader writes WriteInt(targetCharacterId) — op byte consumed by dispatcher.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg.note: PARTY_OPERATION (0x91) op=4 (INVITE). Client sends: Encode1(4)+EncodeStr(targetName). Atlas OperationInvite writes WriteAsciiString(name) — op byte consumed by dispatcher.

calls.comment: v22 = target character name to invite

CWvsContext::OnPartyResult#InviteReject.note: DENY_PARTY_REQUEST (0x92). Synthetic entry: no direct IDA function for the reject path. Wire: Encode1(declineCode)+EncodeStr(inviterName). Atlas InviteReject writes WriteByte(unk)+WriteAsciiString(from). The unk maps to the decline response code; from is the inviter name. Layout matches.

CField::SendJoinPartyMsg#OperationJoin.note: Synthetic entry for OperationJoin (accept party invite). After receiving an invite the client sends partyId to the server. Atlas OperationJoin writes WriteInt(partyId). Wire: Encode4(partyId).

calls.comment: mode byte (error sub-op — no further reads)
nMaxMemberNum (capacity) — atlas writes WriteInt (4 bytes), IDA reads Decode1 (1 byte); REAL BUG
GUILDMEMBER::Decode (37 bytes raw = name[13] + job[4] + level[4] + title[4] + online[4] + signature[4] + allianceTitle[4])
count × charId (4 bytes each, packed via DecodeBuffer)
count × GUILDMEMBER (37 bytes each, packed via DecodeBuffer)
characterId (dispatcher-prefix pattern — consumed by CUserPool before OnGuildNameChanged)
op byte (sub-op dispatcher for GUILD_OPERATION serverbound)
